instances.orginal: The Nile is the longest river in Africa. It flows through several countries, including Egypt and Sudan. The river is an important source of water for agriculture.
instances.simplified:
[1] The Nile is a very long river in Africa.
[2] It goes through Egypt and Sudan.
[3] People use its water for farming.
keyFacts: The Nile is the longest river in Africa.
The Nile flows through several countries.
Egypt and Sudan are among the countries the Nile flows through.
The Nile is an important source of water for agriculture.

instances.orginal:
[1] Marie Curie was a scientist who studied radioactivity.
[2] She won two Nobel Prizes.
[3] Her research contributed to the development of medical X-rays.
instances.simplified: Marie Curie was a scientist who studied radioactivity. She won two Nobel Prizes and helped improve medical X-rays.
keyFacts: Marie Curie won two Nobel Prizes.
Marie Curie helped improve medical X-rays.
